rejected.name: Llama-3.1-ELYZA-JP-70B
rejected.reason: Real model, but never released as open weights, contradicting the task's premise. ELYZA's own Oct 25, 2024 announcement and Japanese press coverage (profab.co.jp) state explicitly '700億パラメータのLlama-3.1-ELYZA-JP-70Bはダウンロードすることはできません' (the 70B model cannot be downloaded) — it is only accessible via ELYZA's demo site and, later, enterprise API/co-development deals. It does not appear anywhere on ELYZA's Hugging Face org page (huggingface.co/elyza), which tops out at 33B-parameter models. Since it was never distributed as downloadable weights, it fails the 'open-weights' criterion and could not be verified as such.

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real model by directly fetching huggingface.co/moonshotai/Kimi-K2-Thinking: verbatim matched 1T total/32B active/384 experts (8 active)/256K context/Modified MIT License and all 5 benchmark scores (HLE-with-tools 44.9, SWE-bench 71.3, AIME25 99.1, GPQA 84.5, BrowseComp 60.2). Release date Nov 6, 2025 corroborated by CNBC and NIST CAISI evaluation coverage. Pricing $0.60/$2.50 per M tokens confirmed via OpenRouter listing.

models.archDisplay: GPT-NeoX / Pythia-style dense transformer

results.notes: HELM core-scenarios mean accuracy at launch; vs LLaMA-7B's 0.465

models.name: GigaChat
models.archDisplay: Proprietary ensemble architecture (NeONKA; undisclosed details)
models.note: Sberbank's Russian ChatGPT rival, launched in closed testing April 24 2023, built on the NeONKA neural-network ensemble; params were never disclosed for the original model. 128K ctx reflects the current GigaChat-2 API (the original 2023 model is deprecated/redirected).
models.verificationNotes: Existence and April 24, 2023 launch date independently reconfirmed via TechStartups, The National, Cyprus Mail and other outlets (all dated 2023-04-24) — original given date was correct. NeONKA architecture name and description ('Neural Omnimodal Network with Knowledge-Awareness', ensemble of models incl. Kandinsky 2.1 for images) independently confirmed via multiple secondary sources — not a hallucination. Could NOT confirm openness/license claim from the cited Wikipedia article itself (it says nothing about licensing); however other sources confirm the original 2023 GigaChat chatbot/API product is closed/proprietary (note: Sber has since open-sourced separate newer 'GigaChat3' MoE checkpoints under MIT in 2025, but that is a distinct later release not covered by this record's release date). 128K context window reconfirmed as current spec for GigaChat-2/2-Pro/2-Max via CloudPrice API listings; original 2023-era first-gen models are now deprecated and redirect to GigaChat-2. No results/benchmark rows were present to verify. Params/activeParams correctly left null as undisclosed for this specific (original, non-MoE) product.

models.name: Starling-7B (alpha)
models.license: Apache-2.0 (with condition not to compete with OpenAI)
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer (Mistral-7B via OpenChat-3.5 base)
models.note: RLAIF fine-tune of OpenChat-3.5/Mistral-7B using the GPT-4-labeled Nectar dataset and APA policy optimization; scored 8.09 MT-Bench (GPT-4 judge) and 91.99 AlpacaEval, best 7B at release behind only GPT-4/GPT-4-Turbo.
models.tpsNote: Architecture-class estimate for a 7B dense model at Q4_K_M GGUF on RTX 4090; not a checkpoint-specific measurement (Mistral's GQA may run somewhat faster in practice).

results.notes: GPT-4-judged MT-Bench; best open 7B at release, behind only GPT-4/GPT-4-Turbo
Self-reported AlpacaEval score listed on the official model card

models.verificationNotes: Existence, org (UC Berkeley/Berkeley NEST), and Nov 20, 2023 release date independently reconfirmed (secondary sources explicitly state 'released on November 20, 2023 by the same UC Berkeley BAIR team'). Fetched the HF model card directly (huggingface.co/berkeley-nest/Starling-LM-7B-alpha) and literally confirmed: license = 'Apache-2.0 license under the condition that the model is not used to compete with OpenAI', base = Openchat-3.5/Mistral-7B-v0.1 (7B params), MT-Bench = 8.09, AlpacaEval = 91.99 — all four values matched the input record exactly, no corrections needed. ctxK=8 consistent with Mistral-7B-v0.1's native 8192-token context. vramQ4Gb/vramFp16Gb/tps4090 are labeled as architecture-class estimates (not vendor-measured), which is appropriate given no checkpoint-specific benchmark was found.
